# cw-analyse

**Warning**: Everything in here is AI generated by the user (skid), Use at your own risk

A web‑based tool to merge, visualise and edit your anime list data – whether you export from HiAnime (cw‑export), your MyAnimeList watchlist, or AniList.  
It combines multiple sources, resolves duplicates, fetches cover images from a local database, and lets you sync changes back to AniList or MAL.

> **Live demo:** [https://cw-analyser.vercel.app](https://cw-analyser.vercel.app)

---

## ✨ Features

- **Import from any source**  
  - `cw-export` JSON (HiAnime scraper)  
  - Watchlist JSON / XML (MAL / AniList exports)  
  - Direct OAuth2 connection to AniList or MyAnimeList

- **Smart merging**  
  Duplicates are merged using the most advanced progress, episode count, and status.

- **Rich media lookup**  
  Uses a local `anime.csv` (generated daily by GitHub Actions) to add cover banners, MAL/AniList IDs, English/Japanese titles.

- **Full editing**  
  Titles, progress (episodes / timestamps), scores (1–10 stars), status, MAL ID linking – all editable live.

- **Bidirectional sync**  
  After editing your unified list, you can push changes back to AniList or MAL (progress, score, status).

- **Share your list**  
  Uploads your list to a Cloudflare Worker; get a short link that expires after 7 days.

- **Stats page**  
  Overview, status donut, score distribution, genre preferences, studio breakdown.

---

## ⚙️ How It Works

### Frontend (`index.html`, `script.js`, `style.css`)
A single‑page application that:
1. Reads uploaded files or connects to an account via OAuth.
2. Merges all entries into a master list (`buildMasterList`).
3. Loads `anime.csv` (if present) to resolve titles and banners.
4. Fetches missing banners from AniList GraphQL and Jikan API.
5. Renders an interactive grid with inline editing.
6. Syncs changes to connected accounts via the respective APIs.

### Cloudflare Worker (`Cloudflare-worker.js`)
Handles three tasks:
- **Share endpoint** – stores list snapshots in KV with 7‑day TTL.
- **MAL token proxy** – exchanges the OAuth2 code for a token (bypasses CORS).
- **MAL API proxy** – forwards authenticated requests to MyAnimeList.

### GitHub Actions (`.github/workflows/sync-dataset.yml`)
Every day at 02:00 UTC, it:
- Downloads the latest `anilist_seasonal_YYYYMMDD.csv` from [LeoRigasaki/Anime-dataset](https://github.com/LeoRigasaki/Anime-dataset).
- Merges new rows into `data.csv` (deduplicated by `anime_id`).
- Trims to a smaller `anime.csv` (keeps only `anime_id, mal_id, title, english_title, user_preferred_title, cover_image_large`).
- Commits and pushes the changes to your repository.

This `anime.csv` is then used by the frontend for lookups.

---

## 🚀 Setup

### 1. Host the frontend
Any static host works (GitHub Pages, Vercel, Netlify, etc.).  
Place `index.html`, `script.js`, `style.css` and the generated `anime.csv` in the same directory.

### 2. Deploy the Cloudflare Worker
- Create a KV namespace named `SHARE_DB`.
- In `wrangler.toml` (or Cloudflare dashboard) bind it to the worker.
- Deploy the code from `Cloudflare-worker.js`.
- Update `SHARE_WORKER_URL` in `script.js` to your worker’s URL.
- Optionally adjust `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` in the worker to your frontend domain.

### 3. Configure GitHub Actions (for the dataset)
- Fork this repository (or create your own).
- Add the workflow file (`.github/workflows/sync-dataset.yml`).
- The workflow will run automatically every day and update `anime.csv`.
- If you need an initial `anime.csv`, run the workflow manually from the Actions tab.

### 4. OAuth credentials
- **AniList** – uses public client ID `39139` (already hardcoded). No further configuration needed.
- **Anilist** – you must obtain your own client ID:
  1. Go to [API Clients · AniList](https://anilist.co/settings/developer).
  2. Create a new client
  3. Set the redirect URI to `https://yourdomain.com/` (the exact path of your frontend).
  4. Replace `AL_CLIENT_ID` in `script.js` with your ID.

- **MyAnimeList** – you must obtain your own client ID:
  1. Go to [MyAnimeList.net – API Clients](https://myanimelist.net/apiconfig).
  2. Create a new client with **App Type = Other**.
  3. Set the redirect URI to `https://yourdomain.com/` (the exact path of your frontend).
  4. Replace `MAL_CLIENT_ID` in `script.js` with your ID.
  5. Make sure your Cloudflare worker’s `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` includes your frontend domain.

---

## 📦 File Structure

```
/
├── index.html                 # Main UI
├── script.js                  # All logic (merging, rendering, sync, OAuth)
├── style.css                  # Styling + mobile layout
├── anime.csv                  # Generated by GitHub Actions – used for lookups
├── data.csv                   # Intermediate merged dataset (not used by frontend)
└── .github/workflows/
    └── sync-dataset.yml       # Daily dataset sync
```

**External files :**
- `Cloudflare-worker.js` – deploy separately as a Cloudflare Worker.

---

## 🛠️ Usage

1. Open the website.
2. **Import your data**:
   - Drag & drop `cw-export.json`, watchlist `.json`/`.xml`, or
   - Click “Connect →” under AniList or MAL and authorise.
3. Press **“ANALYSE LIST”**.
4. Browse the unified grid – search, filter, sort.
5. **Edit any field** (episodes, score, status) directly in the cards.
6. To add a new anime not in your list, search in the “NOT IN LIST” section and set a status – it will be added with a smooth* animation.
7. Use the **↑ Sync** button to push changes to your connected account.
8. Export the final list as JSON / XML / MAL‑import‑ready XML.
9. Share a temporary link with **🔗 Share**.

---

## 🐞 To Fix / Known Issues

> This section lists current limitations or areas that need improvement.

- **Animation of cards** – Currently it is not optimised and may lag alot and has a very glitchy detection
- **MAL sync rate limits** – The worker proxy may hit MAL’s rate limits when syncing large lists. Batch sizes are conservative (10 per batch, 2 concurrent). For very large lists (>500 changes), consider splitting the sync into multiple runs.
- **AniList score format** – The app assumes `POINT_10` (integer 1–10). Other formats (e.g. `POINT_100`, `POINT_10_DECIMAL`) are converted but not thoroughly tested.
- **Title language fallback** – When `anime.csv` lacks an English or Japanese title, the app falls back to the user‑provided title. This may cause mismatches during lookup.
- **Mobile experience** – On narrow screens, card bodies are hidden to save space. The sheet view provides full details, but editing is limited.
- **Worker storage** – KV free tier has a 1 GB limit and a 25 MB per value limit. Large lists (>5 MB) are rejected during sharing.
- **MAL OAuth refresh token** – The worker returns a refresh token, but the frontend does not currently use it to obtain new access tokens automatically. Tokens expire after ~30 days.
- **Performance on huge lists** – Rendering 2000+ cards can be slow. Virtualisation is not implemented. The stats page recomputes everything on open, which may be heavy.
